ddls:
CREATE TABLE "books" (
  "book_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each book */,
  "title" TEXT /* Title of the book */,
  "isbn" TEXT /* ISBN number of the book */,
  "isbn13" TEXT /* ISBN-13 number of the book */,
  "language_code" TEXT /* Language code of the book (e.g., eng for English) */,
  "num_pages" INTEGER /* Number of pages in the book */,
  "publication_date" TEXT /* Publication date of the book */,
  "publisher_id" INTEGER /* ID of the publisher of the book */,
  "edition" TEXT /* Edition of the book */,
  "genre" TEXT /* Genre of the book */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the book */,
  "cover_image" TEXT /* URL of the book's cover image */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the book (e.g., available, out of stock) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("book_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_books_publisher_id FOREIGN KEY ("publisher_id") REFERENCES publishers ("publisher_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "authors" (
  "author_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each author */,
  "author_name" TEXT /* Full name of the author */,
  "biography" TEXT /* Biography of the author */,
  "image" TEXT /* URL of the author's image */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("author_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "book_authors" (
  "book_id" INTEGER /* ID of the book */,
  "author_id" INTEGER /* ID of the author */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the author in the book (e.g., writer, illustrator) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("book_id", "author_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "publishers" (
  "publisher_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each publisher */,
  "publisher_name" TEXT /* Full name of the publisher */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the publisher */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the publisher */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the publisher */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("publisher_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "ratings" (
  "rating_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each rating */,
  "book_id" INTEGER /* ID of the book being rated */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user giving the rating */,
  "rating" REAL /* Rating given by the user (out of 5) */,
  "rating_date" TEXT /* Date the rating was given */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("rating_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_ratings_book_id FOREIGN KEY ("book_id") REFERENCES books ("book_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_ratings_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reviews" (
  "review_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each review */,
  "book_id" INTEGER /* ID of the book being reviewed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user giving the review */,
  "review" TEXT /* Text of the review */,
  "review_date" TEXT /* Date the review was given */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("review_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reviews_book_id FOREIGN KEY ("book_id") REFERENCES books ("book_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reviews_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, publisher, user) */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password of the user (hashed) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "orders" (
  "order_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each order */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user making the order */,
  "order_date" TEXT /* Date the order was made */,
  "total" TEXT /* Total cost of the order */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("order_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_orders_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "order_items" (
  "order_item_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each order item */,
  "order_id" INTEGER /* ID of the order containing the item */,
  "book_id" INTEGER /* ID of the book in the item */,
  "quantity" INTEGER /* Quantity of the book in the item */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("order_item_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_order_items_book_id FOREIGN KEY ("book_id") REFERENCES books ("book_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_order_items_order_id FOREIGN KEY ("order_id") REFERENCES orders ("order_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "analytics" (
  "book_id" INTEGER /* ID of the book */,
  "views" INTEGER /* Number of views for the book */,
  "sales" INTEGER /* Number of sales for the book */,
  "revenue" TEXT /* Total revenue generated by the book */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("book_id")
)
column_names: Language code of the book (e.g., eng for English)
Number of pages in the book
ID of the publisher of the book
URL of the book's cover image
